records.ety: [OE. acheven, OF. achever, achiever, F. achever, to finish; (L. ad) + OF. chief, F. chef, end, head, fr. L. caput head. See Chief.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Achieved (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Achieving (<?/).]

defs: To carry on to a final close; to bring out into a perfected state; to accomplish; to perform; -- as, to achieve a feat, an exploit, an enterprise.
quotes: Supposing faculties and powers to be the same, far more may be achieved in any line by the aid of a capital, invigorating motive than without it.

defs: To obtain, or gain, as the result of exertion; to succeed in gaining; to win.
quotes: Some are born great, some achieve greatness.
Show all the spoils by valiant kings achieved.
He hath achieved a maid That paragons description.
notes: [[Obs]., with a material thing as the aim.]
